skill.name: smiles-de-salter
skill.description: Batch process chemical SMILES strings to remove salt ion portions, retaining only the active core for downstream analysis using RDKit.
skill.summary: Removes salt counterions from chemical SMILES strings using RDKit, retaining only the active pharmaceutical ingredient.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: All 90 static findings are FALSE POSITIVES. The external_commands findings (50) are Markdown code formatting backticks in SKILL.md, not shell execution. The cryptographic findings (16) are RDKit cheminformatics functions (CalcMolFormula, MolFromSmiles) misidentified as encryption. The C2 keyword findings (7) are chemical SMILES carbon bond notation. Path traversal findings (2) are from a security checklist documenting prevention measures. System reconnaissance findings (8) are processing statistics output. Network findings (2) are a legitimate source attribution URL. No malicious intent or obfuscation detected. The skill is a legitimate scientific data analysis tool.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backticks Misidentified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: 50 findings flagged backtick-enclosed text in SKILL.md and references/runtime_checklist.md as Ruby/shell command execution. All instances are standard Markdown formatting for inline code and code blocks showing documentation examples (e.g., python commands, SMILES strings). No actual command execution occurs from these text blocks.

low_findings.title: RDKit Functions Misidentified as Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: 16 findings flagged RDKit cheminformatics function calls (CalcMolFormula, MolFromSmiles, GetNumHeavyAtoms) as weak cryptographic algorithms. These are standard chemical informatics operations for parsing molecular structures, not encryption algorithms.

low_findings.title: Chemical C2 Notation Misidentified as C2 Keyword
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: 7 findings flagged 'C2' in SMILES example strings as Command and Control keywords. In context, C2 is standard chemical SMILES notation representing carbon-carbon bonds or connection table indices. The examples show caffeine and paraxanthine desalting operations.

low_findings.title: Security Checklist Items Misidentified as Path Traversal
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: 2 findings flagged lines referencing '../' as path traversal sequences. These lines appear in a security checklist that explicitly states path traversal is NOT permitted ('No unauthorized file system access (../)' and 'Input file paths validated (no ../ traversal)').

low_findings.title: Source Attribution URL Misidentified as Network Risk
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: 2 findings flagged a GitHub URL as a hardcoded network endpoint. The URL is a legitimate source attribution linking to the project's own GitHub repository. It appears in a markdown citation block and is not used for data exfiltration or any runtime network operation.

low_findings.title: Processing Statistics Misidentified as System Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: 8 findings flagged print statements and documentation as system reconnaissance. The script prints standard processing statistics (total records, success count, invalid SMILES count) after batch operations. Documentation entries describe output discipline and test cases, which are legitimate quality assurance practices.

content.user_title: Remove Salt Ions from Chemical SMILES Strings
content.value_statement: When working with chemical data, SMILES strings often contain salt ions that interfere with downstream analysis. This skill automatically identifies and removes counterions from chemical structures, retaining only the active pharmaceutical ingredient for reproducible research workflows.
actual_capabilities: Process single SMILES strings or batch files (CSV, TSV, SMI)
Identify and remove common inorganic salt ions including Na+, Cl-, and K+
Identify and remove organic acid salt counterions including citrate and tartrate
Retain the largest molecular component as the active core by atom count
Generate structured output files with desalting status for each record
Support keep-largest and keep-all-non-salt component selection modes
limitations: Requires RDKit (version 2022.03.1 or later) to be installed in the execution environment
Cannot distinguish co-crystals from salts; multi-component drugs need manual review
Assumes the active core is the largest molecular component by heavy atom count
Does not support structure normalization or tautomer standardization after desalting

use_cases.title: Pharmaceutical Data Cleaning
use_cases.description: A medicinal chemist needs to clean a dataset of SMILES strings by removing HCl, NaCl, and other salt counterions before structure-activity relationship analysis.

use_cases.title: Chemical Library Standardization
use_cases.description: A data scientist preparing chemical compounds for machine learning models needs to standardize SMILES representations by removing salt portions across a large dataset.

use_cases.title: Drug Discovery Pipeline Integration
use_cases.description: An AI research team processing compound libraries from multiple suppliers needs consistent desalting as a preprocessing step before virtual screening.

prompt_templates.title: Desalt a Single SMILES String
prompt_templates.prompt: Remove the salt ion from this SMILES string and return only the active core: CCO.[Na+]
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick check of a single compound

prompt_templates.title: Process a CSV File of Compounds
prompt_templates.prompt: Process the file compounds.csv using the smiles-de-salter skill. The SMILES data is in a column named 'smiles'. Save the results to desalted_output.csv.
prompt_templates.scenario: Batch processing a CSV with default settings

prompt_templates.title: Custom Column and Output Configuration
prompt_templates.prompt: Run smiles-de-salter on input file library.csv using the column 'CANONICAL_SMILES' for SMILES data. Write output to clean_library.csv and keep only the largest molecular component.
prompt_templates.scenario: Processing a file with non-standard column naming

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Component Retention Mode
prompt_templates.prompt: Process the file mixtures.smi with smiles-de-salter. Instead of keeping only the largest component, retain all non-salt fragments. The input has one SMILES per line with no header.
prompt_templates.scenario: Handling multi-component molecules where all active fragments should be preserved

output_examples.output: The sodium ion was removed. Core SMILES: CCO

output_examples.input: CSV file with 3 compounds: CCO.[Na+], c1ccccc1.[Cl-], CN1C=NC2=C1C(=O)N(C)C(=O)N2C.Cl
output_examples.output: All 3 compounds processed successfully. Output saved to desalted_output.csv with a status column showing desalting results for each record.

best_practices: Always verify a sample of desalted results manually, especially for unfamiliar chemical series where the core may not be the largest fragment.
Run the py_compile validation command before processing large datasets to confirm all dependencies are available.
Review the status column in output files to identify records flagged as invalid SMILES or empty input for quality control.
anti_patterns: Using this skill for reaction prediction or retrosynthesis analysis, which is beyond its designed scope of salt removal only.
Assuming all counterions are removed without verifying the keep_largest parameter behavior for each compound class.
Processing proprietary compound data in environments without appropriate data handling agreements for the specific chemical library.

faq.question: What types of salt ions can this tool remove?
faq.answer: It removes common inorganic salts (NaCl, KCl, HCl, H2SO4) and organic acid salts (citrate, tartrate, maleate) including quaternary ammonium compounds.

faq.question: Does this skill require an internet connection?
faq.answer: No. All processing happens locally using RDKit. No network requests are made during execution.

faq.question: What input file formats are supported?
faq.answer: CSV, TSV, TXT, and SMI/SMILES files are supported. The tool auto-detects the format based on the file extension.

faq.question: How does the tool decide which component is the salt?
faq.answer: It selects the component with the most heavy atoms as the active core. Salt ions are typically smaller, with 3 or fewer heavy atoms.

faq.question: Can I keep multiple non-salt components instead of just the largest?
faq.answer: Yes. Set the keep-largest parameter to false to retain all non-salt components instead of only the largest one.

faq.question: Is RDKit required to run this skill?
faq.answer: Yes. RDKit version 2022.03.1 or later must be installed. It is the core library for parsing and analyzing molecular structures.
